# AI-hint: Machine-readable repository index (llms.txt) for mios-bootstrap — the user-facing installer + AI/knowledge layer of MiOS. Orients an LLM/agent to what MiOS IS (an immutable bootc/OCI Fedora workstation that is also a local, self-replicating agentic AI OS), what this repo contributes to that whole (Phase 0..4 installer, three-layer profile overlay, deployed AI assets), the OpenAI-compatible endpoint contract (Architectural Law 5), and where the canonical files live.
# AI-related: mios-dev, mios-bootstrap, Get-MiOS.ps1, build-mios.sh, install.sh, install.ps1, etc/mios/profile.toml, usr/share/mios/ai/system.md, MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT
# mios-dev/mios-bootstrap

> Machine-readable index for `mios-bootstrap` v0.3.0 — the **interactive
> installer and user-editable layer** of MiOS. User-facing entry point.
> The system layer (Containerfile, FHS overlay, build automation, systemd
> units, Quadlets, kernel args) lives in https://github.com/mios-dev/mios.git
> Both repos overlay onto the same deployed system root.

## What MiOS is (so this index makes sense)

'MiOS' (pronounced *MyOS*) is one thing built two ways at once: an
**immutable, bootc-native Fedora workstation** delivered as a single OCI
image — boot it, `bootc upgrade` it like a `git pull`, `bootc rollback` it
like a Ctrl-Z — that is *also* a **local, self-hosted, self-replicating
agentic AI operating system**. The same image that ships GNOME/Wayland,
NVIDIA + AMD ROCm + Intel iGPU via CDI, KVM/libvirt with VFIO passthrough,
and a k3s + Ceph one-node-cluster path also ships a full local agent stack
behind one OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Because the whole OS is one
rebuildable image, it can reason about itself, drive its own tools, and
effectively re-create itself.

The end-to-end lifecycle is: **build pipeline → OCI image → bootc lifecycle
on the host.** This repository owns the *front of that lifecycle* — the
installer that bootstraps a host into MiOS — plus the AI/knowledge assets
the running agent stack loads. The system overlay it bootstraps (everything
under `usr/`, `etc/`, `srv/`, `var/`) is baked from `mios.git`; this repo
overlays the user-editable layer on top. The load-bearing premise of both
repos: **the repo root IS the deployed system root** — editing a file here
is editing the OS.

## Purpose of this repo

`mios-bootstrap.git` is the **user-facing entry surface**. It turns a bare
Windows or Fedora-bootc host into a MiOS host: preflight, three-layer
profile capture, Total Root Merge of `mios.git` into `/`, build/`bootc
switch`, service stand-up, reboot. It also carries the AI files the deployed
agent plane serves (system prompt, model catalog, MCP registry, RAG
knowledge) and the user profile templates seeded into every account.

## Key files

- `Get-MiOS.ps1`     -- Windows canonical entry (`irm | iex`, one paste, no follow-up prompts)
- `build-mios.sh`    -- Linux/Fedora-bootc canonical entry (`curl | bash`)
- `install.ps1` / `install.sh` -- Phase-0..4 orchestrators (identity capture, Total Root Merge, build, services, reboot)
- `bootstrap.{sh,ps1}`         -- lower-level bootstrap primitives
- `seed-merge.{sh,ps1}`        -- Total Root Merge primitive (Phase-1)
- `etc/mios/profile.toml`             -- Host-layer profile overlay (identity, locale, auth, AI, desktop)
- `etc/skel/.config/mios/`            -- Per-user templates seeded into every uid≥1000 home
- `usr/share/mios/ai/system.md`       -- Deployed agent system prompt (SSOT here; materialized to `/usr/share/mios/ai/` at install)
- `usr/share/mios/ai/models.json`     -- OpenAI `/v1/models` catalog
- `usr/share/mios/ai/mcp.json`        -- MCP server registry
- `usr/share/mios/knowledge/`         -- RAG knowledge graphs
- `mios.toml`                         -- This repo's reference `mios.toml` (the singular SSOT)
- `AGENTS.md`                         -- Canonical agent entry point (agents.md standard); `CLAUDE.md` / `GEMINI.md` redirect to it

## Install commands

Windows (PowerShell / `WinKey+R`, no pre-setup required):
```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mios-dev/mios-bootstrap/main/Get-MiOS.ps1 | iex"
```

Linux (Fedora / bootc host):
```bash
sudo bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mios-dev/mios-bootstrap/main/build-mios.sh)"
```

Already on a Fedora-bootc-compatible host:
```bash
bootc switch ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest && sudo systemctl reboot
```

The `irm | iex` / `curl | bash` shape is the entry contract — runnable with
no pre-existing setup. `Get-MiOS.ps1` self-cache-busts, two-pass
self-elevates, provisions `M:\`, clones both repos, stands up the `MiOS-DEV`
podman machine, then **stops** — the build is operator-triggered via
`mios build`. Each interactive prompt auto-accepts its `mios.toml` default
after **90 seconds** idle (`MIOS_PROMPT_TIMEOUT=0` waits forever, `1` is
fastest unattended).

## Repo split (never double-track paths)

`mios.git` owns: Containerfile, build automation, FHS system overlay,
systemd units, Quadlet sidecars, kernel args, tmpfiles, sysusers.

`mios-bootstrap.git` owns: AI files (`usr/share/mios/ai/`), knowledge
graphs, user profile templates, installer scripts. The two overlay onto the
same `/`; no path is tracked in both.

## `mios.toml` — the singular SSOT

Every operator-tunable value in the entire pipeline (packages, ports, AI
lanes, services, identity, theme, Quadlet enables) lives in `mios.toml`,
resolved through a three-layer overlay (highest wins):

```
~/.config/mios/profile.toml      # per-user (seeded from /etc/skel)
/etc/mios/profile.toml           # host/admin
/usr/share/mios/profile.toml     # vendor defaults (lowest)
```

Edited as HTML in a local browser (`file://` works — no server), saved to
`%USERPROFILE%\Downloads\mios.toml` (Windows) or `~/.config/mios/mios.toml`
(Linux), then fetched by the pipeline. Empty strings do NOT override
non-empty values below; an empty user TOML is the vendor-default state, not
an error. Hardcoded values that could live in `mios.toml` are bugs.

## AI stack (what this repo's AI files feed)

The deployed AI plane is the "agentic AI OS" half of MiOS. A request flows
from a front-end (Open WebUI, the chat/Discord gateways, the `mios` CLI)
into the **agent-pipe** orchestrator (`:8640`) — router + refine +
council/swarm fan-out + critic/polish — which fronts **MiOS-Hermes**
(`:8642`), the OpenAI-compatible agent gateway that owns sessions, the
tool-loop, skills, and browser control; a **prefilter** (`:8641`) injects
fan-out hints on decomposable prompts. Tools are called over **MCP** and
peer agents reached over **A2A**.

Inference lanes are named by *function*, not by upstream tool:

- **`mios-llm-light`** (`:11450`) — the **primary** lane: a `llama.cpp`
  multi-model server fronted by the `llama-swap` proxy image
  (`ghcr.io/mostlygeek/llama-swap`). Auto-swaps the everyday chat/reasoning
  models behind one endpoint, KV-pages each conversation to disk, **and**
  serves embeddings (`nomic-embed-text`, OpenAI-compat `/v1/embeddings`)
  plus the `mios-opencode` coder model. Map:
  `usr/share/mios/llamacpp/llama-swap.yaml` (in `mios.git`).
- **`mios-llm-heavy`** (`:11441`, served-name `mios-heavy`) — heavy GPU lane
  (SGLang), gated off by default on VRAM grounds.
- **`mios-llm-heavy-alt`** — alternate heavy lane (vLLM), likewise gated.
- **`mios-llm-worker@`** — single-model swarm workers for fan-out.

Agent memory is **PostgreSQL + pgvector** (the `mios-pgvector` container on
`:5432`): agent memory, events, tool calls, sessions, skills, scratch, and a
`knowledge` table of finished Q+A with vector recall (embeddings from
`nomic-embed-text` on `mios-llm-light`). `web_search` is backed by a local
**SearXNG** (`:8888`); the coder peer is served through the
**opencode-gateway** (`:8633`).

Every agent and tool resolves the single endpoint via `MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT`
(Architectural Law 5) — any OpenAI-API-compatible editor/CLI talks to the
same local brain, no vendor account in the loop. The lanes speak the
OpenAI/Ollama-compatible API (a legitimate upstream API-compat reference),
but the *engines* are `llama.cpp`/SGLang/vLLM, not a hosted service.

## Standards

- OpenAI REST API v1 at `MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT` (no vendor-cloud endpoints, no vendor-specific agent/dev-tool product names)
- Linux FHS 3.0 and XDG Base Directory spec
- systemd / Podman Quadlet (unprivileged)
- bootc / OCI image format

## Architectural Laws (the contract that keeps MiOS immutable AND agentic)

1. **USR-OVER-ETC** — static config in `/usr/lib/<component>.d/`; `/etc/` is admin-override only.
2. **NO-MKDIR-IN-VAR** — every `/var/` path declared via `usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/*.conf`; never written at build time.
3. **BOUND-IMAGES** — every Quadlet image symlinked into `/usr/lib/bootc/bound-images.d/` and baked into the image (the AI containers ship *inside* the host).
4. **BOOTC-CONTAINER-LINT** — final `RUN` of the `Containerfile`. Fail the lint, fail the build.
5. **UNIFIED-AI-REDIRECTS** — every agent/tool targets `MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT`. No vendor-hardcoded URLs.
6. **UNPRIVILEGED-QUADLETS** — every Quadlet declares `User=`, `Group=`, `Delegate=yes`. Documented exceptions: `mios-ceph`, `mios-k3s`, `mios-forgejo-runner` (rationale in their headers).

Laws 1–4 keep the image deterministic, atomic, and self-contained so bootc
can upgrade/roll it back; Laws 5–6 keep the AI plane unified and
least-privileged so the agent stack stays portable and sandboxed.

## Status

MiOS is in active development at `v0.3.0`. The migration off the early
Ollama / legacy-datastore / Qdrant stack is complete: inference + embeddings now
run on the `mios-llm-light` lane (`:11450`) with gated heavy GPU lanes
(`mios-llm-heavy` / `mios-llm-heavy-alt`), and the unified agent datastore is
PostgreSQL + pgvector (`mios-pgvector`). The retired `CloudWS` naming is gone
— every shipped artifact is `mios-<component>`. Ollama survives only as an
upstream *API-compat reference* (the lanes speak the OpenAI/Ollama-compatible
API) and in historical migration notes — not as a live MiOS backend.

## License

Apache-2.0. Component licenses for every shipped piece are catalogued in
`mios.git` at `usr/share/doc/mios/reference/licenses.md`.

The `'MiOS'` name (capitalized) is a project mark; lowercase `mios` (used in
file paths, package names, env-var prefixes) is the technical identifier and
free of that constraint.
